pages.raw_text:
Not having been cared FOR in the
proposed city charter as far as it
has been written, members Of the
police and fire departments yester-
day announced their intention to
fight the charter If it should come
before the people in its present
form.


Earlier in the stage of the DELHI
eratiOns of the charter revision
commission selected by the mayor
the question of a civil service com
mission was considered and yo;ei
down, and the charter was left
Without any provisions along that
me. The subcommittee that pre-
pared the final draft of the charter
now being considered by the com
mission has added no clause look
ing to the retention of the present
Police or fire departments, and
Aencc the kick.


At the present time there is a
civil service provision concerning
the discharge of policemen and fire
men, but none regarding how or
in what name" they shall be
hired. In this way politicians can
fill the police force or fire depart
nent with their favorites, but they
cannot be removed without the fill
ing of charges and trial before
the police or fire boards.


1t seems that the matter of the
relation of the police to the pres
ent charter was sleeping quietly
until the last meeting Of the com
mission this week, when the draft
of the charter as thus far adopted
was placed in the vault at the pO'
! lice department for safe keeping
The following day, when the copy
of the proposed charter was called
for. the police had it inspected fol
its position in relation to the pc
lice department, but nothing with
in the limits of the charter was
found on the subject. A quiet
storm of protest was then started
and it has grown right along evel
since, until there are a number Of
hammers" in motion for the pro
POSED constitution laws of the mu
nicipality.


Chief of Police Sullivan say:
that he thinks the necessity fol
police hostility to the charter will
not present itself, for the reason
that two members of the commits
sion have informed him that some
provision for the protection of the
police will be inserted.
